top_gap_prompt: Which web analytics software should a SaaS founder or marketing manager use?
category_long: privacy-first web analytics for SaaS founders

gaps.title: Thin or missing dedicated competitor-comparison pages
gaps.description: Plausible lacks depth on '[brand] vs [competitor]' comparison pages, making it less likely to appear when AI models answer alternative/comparison queries.

gaps.title: No Organization or SoftwareApplication schema on homepage
gaps.description: Plausible's homepage lacks structured data (schema.org/Organization or SoftwareApplication), reducing the chance AI models pull a rich product snippet when answering category-discovery queries.

gaps.title: Low placement frequency in 'best [category] tools' roundups
gaps.description: Plausible is under-represented in third-party 'best web analytics' listicles and review articles. AI models heavily weight these sources in category-discovery responses.

gap_prompts.prompt_text: Which web analytics software should a SaaS founder or marketing manager use?
gap_prompts.why_it_matters: Discovery queries drive early-funnel awareness. Buyers use these to find solutions to problems they haven't yet named. Missing here means you're invisible before the consideration stage.

gap_prompts.prompt_text: web analytics tools with native WordPress integration
gap_prompts.why_it_matters: Integration queries signal technical buyers in the evaluation stage. Missing here loses decision-stage opportunities to tools that have stronger integration documentation.
Alternative searches are used by buyers actively switching tools. Visibility here captures switching-intent demand at its peak.
